message:
fix(redaction): --allow-secret should downgrade block to warn, not disable scanning

The --allow-secret flag was incorrectly setting mode to "off" which
disables ALL scanning. This is more dangerous than intended.

The bead spec says --allow-secret is an "explicit override to bypass
block" mode - meaning it should still detect and log findings, just
not fail the operation.

Changed: --allow-secret now downgrades "block" to "warn" only when
the current mode is actually "block". This way:
- Secrets are still detected and logged
- Operations proceed without blocking
- Users are aware of what was found

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
